column1: NGR is based on the relationship to the church, however, Eastwood Colliery was located 1.5km S at SK460459 but closed in the early 1880s, there is no mention of the gauge moving althought the elevation on the 1860s and 1870s record are more consistent with the Colliery location and do not have realtionship information to the church.

The 1868 readings and Jan 1869 readings are duplicates of Derby All Saints, which is 10 miles away and there is no obvious connection between the observers. Given that the Derby All Saints record is a more comprehensive record it is most probably this Eastwood record that has copied the other record.
This gauge is at 3ft above ground level
